title: UPS Workers Approve $30 Billion Labor Contract, Averting Strike
text: Workers at UPS (UPS) approved a five-year contract worth $30 billion in wage increases and other benefits, ending contentious negotiations and the threat of a strike.

title: UPS strikes averted as workers accept pay deal
text: Parcel delivery giant UPS has seen off threats of strikes after workers overwhelmingly accepted a new five-year labour deal from the company. Some 86.3% of Teamsters union members who voted backed the deal, which will see average driver salaries lifted to around $170,000 (£134,564) from roughly $140,000.

title: UPS workers ratify 5-year contract, averting potentially crippling strike
text: The deal raises pay and eliminates a two-tier wage system for drivers at Atlanta-based UPS, the world's largest package delivery company, which handles about a quarter of US parcel deliveries.

title: Teamsters Approve New UPS Contract, Ending Strike Threat
text: More than 86% of workers were in favor of the deal with the shipping giant.

title: UPS Workers Avert Strike by Approving New Contract
text: The vote by members of the Teamsters union removes a potential threat to the economy.

title: Teamsters union votes to ratify UPS contract
text: The Teamsters union members overwhelmingly voted to ratify the new five-year collective bargaining agreement with UPS after months of negotiations and concerns over a potential strike.

title: UPS workers approve massive new labor deal with big raises
text: The agreement is the largest collective bargaining agreement at a private-sector employer based on number of workers.

title: UPS, Teamsters Await Vote on New Labor Pact

title: UPS strike threat isn't over: Teamsters will announce today whether UPS workers approve a new contract
text: On Tuesday, the Teamsters union will announce if their 340,000 members at UPS have ratified a new contract or if they rejected it, reviving the risk of a crippling nationwide strike.

title: UPS stock drops after revenue miss, lowered outlook amid volume impact from labor negotiations
text: Shares of United Parcel Service Inc. UPS, +0.67% dropped 5.6% toward a six-week low in premarket trading Tuesday, after the package delivery giant reported second-quarter revenue that fell short of expectations and cut its full-year outlook, citing the volume impact from labor negotiations. Net income fell to $2.08 billion, or $2.42 a share, from $2.85 billion, or $3.25 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: UPS Releases 2Q 2023 Earnings
text: ATLANTA--(BUSINESS WIRE)--UPS (NYSE:UPS) today announced second-quarter 2023 consolidated revenues of $22.1 billion, a 10.9% decrease from the second quarter of 2022. Consolidated operating profit was $2.8 billion, down 21.4% compared to the second quarter of 2022, and down 18.4% on an adjusted basis. Diluted earnings per share were $2.42 for the quarter; adjusted diluted earnings per share of $2.54 were 22.8% below the same period in 2022. For the second quarter of 2023, GAAP results included.
